meta.title: Hedge Fund AI Pipeline — Investor Risk & Return Matrix (Reality-Adjusted)
meta.compounding_note: Monthly profit calculated on INITIAL capital each month. Balance grows linearly. Position size does not scale with growing balance. This is the conservative and auditable model.
meta.reality_adjustment_note: Parameters calibrated to account for: API downtime (1-9%), signal latency degradation, order fill slippage, regime filter utilisation, correlation clustering, data quality failures, and error haircut. tradesMonth represents COMPLETED positions after all pipeline filters — not raw signal count.
meta.benchmark_context: Renaissance Medallion net: ~40%/yr. Two Sigma: 15-20%/yr. Top retail algo: 20-40%/yr. Our system targets 22-155%/yr depending on infrastructure tier — achievable through AI information advantage, not leverage.
meta.disclaimer: Illustrative projections only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results. All figures are pre-tax and pre-fee.

configuration.compounding: Monthly profit fixed on initial capital. Balance = capital + (monthly_profit × month_number). No position scaling.
reality_factors_applied: Signal latency degradation (12ms–2800ms depending on tier)
Order fill rate (N/A paper → 94% Full Institutional)
Regime filter utilisation (58–80% — system sits out wrong-environment days)
Correlation clustering penalty (9–24% — correlated signals counted as one)
Data quality scrub (87–97% — stale/corrupt data discards)
Error factor (57–79% — composite noise, model overfitting, unexpected events)
Slippage (11–48 bps — higher at lower tiers due to market orders and L1 data)
tradesMonth is completed positions AFTER all filters (10–16/month)

reality_profile.description: Paper trading environment. Free-tier APIs only. All signals are validated manually before execution. System learns the market before risking capital.
reality_profile.notes: MVP is intentionally conservative. The 52% win rate reflects real live-equivalent paper signals after the 72% AI confidence gate. Not suitable for live capital.

reality_profile.description: First live IBKR fills. Real slippage and fill uncertainty now present. L1 market data only — no order book depth. News arrives 2-5 seconds late.
latency_sources: L1 data — no depth of book
reality_profile.notes: Real fills introduce slippage not present in paper mode. 14 completed trades/month reflects ~3-4 qualified signals per week, with ~35% filtered out by confidence gate and regime check.

reality_profile.description: Polygon.io L2 order book live. Regime detection classifying market state every 15 minutes. Correlation guard prevents clustered entries. Meaningful infrastructure advantage begins here.
reality_profile.notes: L2 data provides genuine edge on entry timing. Regime filter removes approximately 32% of candidate signals on wrong-environment days (e.g. high-VIX chop).

reality_profile.description: Complete 11-node pipeline. Options Greeks via ORATS. RL execution node makes limit/market decisions. Full audit trail. Investor-grade infrastructure. System behaves like a small institutional desk.
downtime_sources: ORATS Greeks refresh delay during high-IV events
Databento tick gaps on illiquid instruments
latency_sources: VPS co-location partial (same datacenter, not rack)
reality_profile.notes: The RL execution node replaces naive market orders, reducing slippage from 3.8bps to 2.2bps. Options Greeks integration allows filtering trades where IV is unfavorable, removing a significant class of false positives.

reality_profile.description: Dedicated co-located VPS in same datacenter as IBKR matching engine. Multi-asset coverage across equities, futures, and FX. Bloomberg Terminal alternative data stack. Proprietary signal layer built on 6+ months of live P&L data.
downtime_sources: Multi-asset correlation spikes during macro events
reality_profile.notes: At this tier, the system has accumulated 6+ months of live trading data enabling proprietary signal refinement. Slippage drops to 1.6bps through intelligent order scheduling. 16 trades/month represents ~0.8 qualified setups per day across all instruments.

reality_profile.description: Nanosecond-precision tick data via Databento L3. HFT-lite order routing. Full hot-standby redundancy with automatic failover. Maximum edge achievable at the retail/institutional boundary. 15 trades/month is lower than lower tiers because the confidence gate is strictest — only the highest-conviction setups are taken.
downtime_sources: Databento L3 feed reconnects (avg 2 events/week, <30s each)
Multi-jurisdiction compliance pauses during news blackouts
reality_profile.notes: Full Institutional is the ceiling of the retail algorithmic stack. The lower trade count (15/month vs 16 at lower tiers) is by design — at this tier the system is extremely selective, accepting only the top 8-10% of all signals that pass the 72% confidence gate. Quality over quantity.
